frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, left needle driver supporting suture thread
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good initial needle grip position, appears to be at 2/3 point from tip

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments coordinating to orient needle perpendicular to needle driver
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Internal rotation technique being used to adjust needle angle to 90 degrees

frame_analyses.description: Needle being pushed through tissue with proper perpendicular approach
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Following needle curvature, rotating instrument to avoid tissue injury

frame_analyses.description: Needle has passed through tissue, left instrument supporting on far side
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle path following curvature, adequate tissue bite

frame_analyses.description: Thread being pulled through tissue with sufficient tail length visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintaining proper support to avoid tissue tearing

frame_analyses.description: C-loop formation beginning for surgeon's knot double throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Left instrument forming C-shaped loop, right instrument approaching with thread

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument passed through C-loop, grasping tail for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper tip-to-tip instrument positioning for double throw

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot double throw) being completed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both instruments working together to tighten the double throw

frame_analyses.description: Second C-loop formation for single throw in opposite direction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand positions switched for alternating direction throw

frame_analyses.description: Final single throw being formed with hand position switched again
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper alternating hand technique maintained throughout

knot_assessments.notes: Proper double throw technique with good tension
Single throw in opposite direction, proper hand switching
Final single throw with alternating hands maintained

confidence_rationale: Clear video quality with good visualization of needle placement, knot formation, and hand switching. All critical elements well-documented throughout the procedure.
technique_summary: Expert-level performance demonstrating textbook intracorporeal suturing technique with proper needle loading, precise placement, and flawless knot sequence with consistent hand alternation.
strengths: Excellent completion time of 52 seconds (expert benchmark 80-120s)
Perfect suture placement with no visible deviation from marks
Proper surgeon's knot followed by two alternating single throws
No tissue damage or drain gaps
improvement_suggestions: Performance is already at expert level with minimal room for improvement
